definition: The fourth of the five heaps: saṅkhārā considered in the heap-family sense rather than as the dependent-arising link or the whole class of conditioned things.
notes: This compound is one of the key drift points in the cluster. The project keeps heap of putting-together activities as the default because it explicitly marks the heap-context override recorded at the saṅkhārā headword. Formations heap remains a comparative alternate for brief explanatory prose, but it should not silently replace the house rendering in direct translation. Do not import the dependent-arising link sense here by habit. SN 22.48 and SN 22.59 support this heap-context handling.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose using familiar aggregate terminology

version_notes: Expanded to encode the heap-context override for saṅkhārā explicitly at the constituent-compound level.

authority_basis.scope: Supports the term's use in the standard heap and not-self formulas.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'heap of putting-together activities' as the default rendering for the compound unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not import the dependent-arising link sense or revert to volitional-formations language without an explicit local reason.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents one of the highest-risk context shifts in the five-heaps family.
